title: Add --noout option to prevent the output of ids
body:
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11515

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

This looks hard to maintain, we always have to remember to honour this option when we are adding new output.

I think it would be simpler if we would make this a global option and use dup2 to set `/dev/null` as stdout.
I'm a bit confused by this change.  Is it suppressing all IDs throughout?  I.e. image IDs, container id, volume id, manifest id, etc?
The request was to have no stdout Basically the equivalent of podman * > /dev/null

Could we call it `--quiet`?

At the moment `--quiet` is an option for the pull command, but we are adding it to the main podman scope.

`--noout` as implemented in the PR will also cause `pull --quiet` since we are silencing every write to stdout.

--quiet in the case of pull will quiet the output of progress, which actually happens on stderr.  This --noout option would stop the output of the container id, which podman pull --quiet does not.
podman --noout pull ...

Will show the progress but no id.

podman --noout pull --quiet ...

Will stop progress and the output of ID.

> podman --noout pull --quiet ...

would be better to have `podman --quiet=WHAT pull --quiet`?

For now, it can accept just `stdout` that is also the default.  We can extend it later to accept `stderr` or `both`.

I'm a little iffy on that - last time we changed a boolean flag to a not-boolean flag (`--systemd=always` being allowed) we definitely broke some people. We *can* if we really want to (4.0 release and all) but I would kind of prefer to avoid that
@mheon Sure, although 4.0 is the time to break stuff.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
LGTM, this will fix the scp -q issue as well.
